steps.title: Check the mean
steps.content: Every value in data set F is between $120$ and $230$. Adding $15$, which is below every original value, must pull the mean down. Statement I must be true.

steps.title: Check the median
steps.content: Data set F has $57$ values, so its median is the $29$th value. After adding $15$, data set G has $58$ values, and its median is the average of the old $28$th and $29$th values. If those two values are equal, the median does not decrease. Statement II is not guaranteed.

steps.title: Select the true statement
steps.content: Only Statement I must be less for data set G than for data set F. The answer is A.
